iraola.summary: High-intensity, vertical 'controlled chaos'. Base 4-2-3-1 (flexes to 4-3-3). Signature is a hybrid press — zonal shape that springs man-to-man traps — plus an aggressive high line, immediate counterpress and direct, wide attacking. Among the most physically demanding systems in Europe (Bournemouth led the PL for distance covered and lowest PPDA).
needs: Ball-playing libero: calm vs the press, brave off his line, accurate short and long.
Front-foot defenders who step into midfield and recover quickly behind a high line; line-breaking passers.
Tireless two-way engines that stretch build-up then attack the byline; second-ball hunters.
Do-everything engine room: beat the press, win second balls, counterpress instantly. Athleticism non-negotiable.
Pace, 1v1 directness and aggressive off-ball running/pressing.
Thrive in pockets and on second balls; striker runs in behind, holds up and floods the box.

squad.name: Alisson Becker
squad.fitNote: Ideal as Iraola's spare-man libero — elite distribution short and long, calm under the press and brave off his line.
squad.blurb: No.1 goalkeeper. Sweeper-keeper with elite distribution; the platform for a high defensive line.
squad.outlook: Stays as undisputed first choice after a Juventus U-turn; key to making Iraola's high line work.

wc.note: Brazil are out — did not reach the semi-finals (Spain, France, England, Argentina). Tournament over.

squad.name: Giorgi Mamardashvili
squad.fitNote: Modern ball-playing keeper whose profile suits the system; high-quality competition and long-term option.
squad.blurb: Deputy goalkeeper signed from Valencia; commanding and good with his feet.
squad.outlook: Cup keeper and long-term heir to Alisson; pushes for more minutes in 2026/27.

wc.note: Georgia did not qualify

squad.name: Armin Pécsi
squad.fitNote: Young third-choice developing his game; one for the future.
squad.blurb: Young Hungarian third-choice goalkeeper, signed as a prospect.
squad.outlook: Development year; likely cup-squad or loan to build experience.

wc.note: Hungary did not qualify

squad.name: Virgil van Dijk (c)
squad.fitNote: Perfect as the line-breaking ball-playing CB ('Senesi role'); the only question is sustaining pace behind a very high line at 34.
squad.blurb: Captain and defensive leader; reads the game, starts attacks with raking passes.
squad.outlook: Remains the on-pitch leader; managing his minutes and the high line are the season's balancing act.

wc.note: Netherlands OUT — beaten by Morocco on penalties (1-1) in the Round of 32; Van Dijk captained throughout. Tournament over.

squad.name: Jérémy Jacquet
squad.fitNote: Athletic, front-foot CB who can also cover RB — suits the aggressive stepping the system asks of defenders.
squad.blurb: New £55m centre-back from Rennes; quick, aggressive and comfortable defending space.
squad.outlook: Arrives as a long-term CB; back in full training after shoulder surgery and could push Gomez for the No.2 CB spot.

squad.name: Joe Gomez
squad.fitNote: Genuinely versatile (CB, RB, even LB) and athletic, but his injury record makes him valuable depth rather than a system cornerstone.
squad.blurb: Versatile defender who covers all across the back line; pace is his asset.
squad.outlook: Squad utility piece across the back four — value depends on staying fit.

wc.note: Not selected

squad.name: Giovanni Leoni
squad.fitNote: Composed, progressive young CB who can cover right-back; profile fits but he is unproven at this intensity.
squad.blurb: Highly rated teenage centre-back signed from Parma; ball-playing defender.
squad.outlook: Development depth; minutes in cups or a loan likely once fit.
squad.injury: ACL rupture (right knee, surgery Oct 2025) — late-stage rehab, back in outdoor work at the AXA and nearing a return

squad.name: Jeremie Frimpong
squad.fitNote: Best as an attacking RB but can play RW; blistering pace and run-timing are tailor-made for the role, with balance/injury the caveat.
squad.blurb: Flying right-back/wing-back; relentless overlaps and runs in behind.
squad.outlook: First-choice attacking RB; his pace is central to Iraola's wide overloads if he stays fit.

wc.note: Left out of NED squad (injury record)

squad.name: Conor Bradley
squad.fitNote: More defensively sound RB (can fill in at LB); high work-rate and pace fit the press and give balance when Frimpong flies forward.
squad.blurb: Energetic two-way full-back; defensively reliable with a good engine.
squad.outlook: Rotates with Frimpong and offers a more balanced option; fitness after a knee issue is the watch.

wc.note: Did not qualify

squad.name: Calvin Ramsay
squad.fitNote: Fringe RB depth who has struggled for minutes and fitness.
squad.blurb: Back-up right-back; attacking instincts but injury-hit career so far.
squad.outlook: Likely a loan to rebuild rhythm.

squad.name: Milos Kerkez
squad.fitNote: Arguably the single best fit in the squad — produced his best football under Iraola at Bournemouth as a marauding, second-ball-hunting LB.
squad.blurb: Marauding left-back; tireless up-and-down the flank, loves the byline.
squad.outlook: First-choice LB reunited with the coach who got the most out of him — a key man for 2026/27.

wc.note: Hungary did not qualify

squad.name: Ryan Gravenberch
squad.fitNote: Best as the deep-lying carrier in the pivot; driving runs and physicality are exactly what Iraola wants. Also fine as a box-to-box 8.
squad.blurb: Press-resistant midfield engine; carries the ball through lines.
squad.outlook: Cornerstone of the double pivot; one of the biggest beneficiaries of the new system.

wc.note: Netherlands OUT in the Round of 32 (lost to Morocco on penalties after a 1-1 draw). Tournament over.

squad.name: Wataru Endo
squad.fitNote: A specialist screener (can emergency-cover CB); lacks the two-way range and progression Iraola's pivot demands, so better as a game-state sub.
squad.blurb: Experienced defensive midfielder; aggressive ball-winner and leader.
squad.outlook: Squad depth and a closing-out option rather than a first-choice fit.
squad.injury: Left ankle/foot ligament injury (Feb 2026, surgery; aggravated in May) — out of action

wc.note: Withdrew from Japan's World Cup squad injured and retired from international football (11 Jun).

squad.name: Stefan Bajcetic
squad.fitNote: Right mobile, ball-winning profile for the pivot (can also cover CB), but injuries leave him as long-term depth until fit and trusted.
squad.blurb: Talented young pivot; mobile ball-winner whose career has been stalled by injury.
squad.outlook: If finally fit, an in-house option in the No.6 role; otherwise a loan could help.

squad.name: Dominik Szoboszlai
squad.fitNote: Excellent as a right-sided 8 or as the 10; very good on the right wing and has even covered RB. Receives between lines, shoots from range and presses ferociously — best in central/right-of-centre roles.
squad.blurb: All-action, versatile midfielder; box-to-box runs, long-range shooting and relentless pressing.
squad.outlook: A coach's dream for this system — likely a near-ever-present, most often as a right-sided 8.

wc.note: Hungary did not qualify

squad.name: Alexis Mac Allister
squad.fitNote: Best as a deeper playmaking 8; technically and tactically superb, but the pivot's constant pressing/transition load tests his physical output over 90 minutes — and Madrid interest lingers.
squad.blurb: World Cup-winning midfield brain; press-resistant passer who dictates tempo.
squad.outlook: Quality is unquestioned; his role and future (Madrid links) are the season's question marks.

wc.note: RUNNER-UP — Mac Allister starred as Argentina stunned England 2-1 in the semi (hit the post before Lautaro's stoppage-time winner), but Argentina lost the final 1-0 to Spain after extra time (Ferran Torres 106'). Tournament over.

squad.name: Curtis Jones
squad.fitNote: Best as a two-way 8; energetic and press-resistant, can fill in at right-back or play a bit deeper. Inter interest is the variable.
squad.blurb: Local academy midfielder; tidy on the ball, aggressive off it.
squad.outlook: Useful rotation in midfield — if he stays past Inter interest.

wc.note: Not selected

squad.name: Trey Nyoni
squad.fitNote: Highly rated teenage 8/10; squad depth and a development prospect for now.
squad.blurb: Exciting academy midfielder; composed passer beyond his years.
squad.outlook: Cup minutes and continued development.

squad.name: Florian Wirtz
squad.fitNote: Best as the central 10, where his pockets-and-chaos game thrives; also effective as a left-sided creator or advanced 8. After a tough debut year the system should unlock him.
squad.blurb: Marquee creative playmaker; finds pockets, threads passes and links everything.
squad.outlook: The system is built to free him — a bounce-back, talisman season is the expectation.

wc.note: Germany OUT — eliminated in the early knockout rounds. Tournament over for Wirtz.

squad.name: Alexander Isak
squad.fitNote: A 'dream No.9' for the system — runs in behind, holds up, finishes coolly and links play. Pure central striker.
squad.blurb: British-record centre-forward; silky, lethal and a perfect transition striker.
squad.outlook: The focal point of the attack — a big goal return is expected now he's fit.

wc.note: Sweden OUT — beaten 3-0 by France in the Round of 32. Isak finished with 1 goal and 3 assists in 4 games. Tournament over.

squad.name: Hugo Ekitiké
squad.fitNote: Best as a mobile central striker but can play off the left; runs channels and presses — a strong alternative/partner to Isak when fit.
squad.blurb: Last season's top scorer; mobile, direct forward who stretches defences.
squad.outlook: Returning from a serious Achilles injury — timeline, not talent, is the question.
squad.injury: Ruptured Achilles — long-term, rehab into start of 2026/27

wc.note: Out injured (Achilles)

squad.name: Jayden Danns
squad.fitNote: Academy striker depth with a poacher's instinct; needs minutes.
squad.blurb: Homegrown striker; sharp movement in the box.
squad.outlook: Cup minutes or a loan to develop.

squad.name: Cody Gakpo
squad.fitNote: Best cutting in from the left, and can lead the line; effective as a wide target-man combining with an overlapping Kerkez, but the system's demand for repeated pace/directness is a question — and Spurs are circling.
squad.blurb: Powerful left-sided forward; strong, two-footed, scores and creates.
squad.outlook: A key attacker if he stays, but a big Tottenham bid could test the club's resolve.

wc.note: Netherlands OUT in the Round of 32 — Gakpo scored in the 1-1 draw with Morocco but the Dutch lost on penalties. Tournament over.

squad.name: Federico Chiesa
squad.fitNote: Two-footed wide forward who can play either flank or through the middle; directness suits the brief but durability is the concern and the club wants faster wingers.
squad.blurb: Experienced, direct wide forward; carries a goal threat when fit.
squad.outlook: Squad option whose minutes hinge on fitness and incoming wingers.

wc.note: Italy did not qualify

squad.name: Rio Ngumoha
squad.fitNote: Natural left winger (right-footed, cutting inside) who can switch to the right; an isolate-and-attack dribbler whose pace and 1v1 ability fit perfectly, with youth the only caveat — a real opportunity post-Salah.
squad.blurb: Teenage winger and the club's youngest-ever scorer; fearless dribbler.
squad.outlook: Breakout chance with Salah gone — set for far more first-team minutes.

squad.name: Kaide Gordon
squad.fitNote: Talented wide forward whose progress has been badly hampered by injuries; depth for now.
squad.blurb: Once-prodigious winger trying to rebuild after injuries.
squad.outlook: Needs a clean run of fitness; a loan is possible.

squad.name: Víctor Muñoz
squad.fitNote: Direct, transition-focused winger who can play across the front line; pace and 1v1 dribbling fit Iraola's isolate-and-attack plan, with the usual adaptation question for a first season in England.
squad.blurb: Summer 2026's first signing — 22-year-old Spain winger from Osasuna (ex-Real Madrid); quick and dangerous in transition.
squad.outlook: Arrives to refresh the wide areas post-Salah; a six-year deal signals a long-term bet, and he should compete for minutes on either flank straight away.

injuries.status: Long-term — progressing (light passing work); will miss pre-season, rehab into the start of 2026/27
Reported for preparatory work at the AXA but set to miss the start of pre-season; not yet cleared for team training
Set to miss the start of pre-season; recovery ongoing

injuries.issue: Left ankle/foot ligament injury (Feb 2026 surgery, aggravated in May)
injuries.status: Out of action; withdrew from Japan's World Cup squad and retired internationally (11 Jun)
Deal official (joined 1 Jul); back in team training and fit for pre-season
Late-stage rehab — back doing outdoor work at the AXA and nearing a return, but likely to miss the very start of pre-season
Recovered — back training and played at the World Cup

worldcup.status: TOURNAMENT OVER — Spain are 2026 World Cup champions, beating Argentina 1-0 after extra time in the final (19 Jul, New York/New Jersey; Ferran Torres 106'). Argentina had stunned England 2-1 in the semi (15 Jul) with Alexis Mac Allister outstanding — the last Liverpool man standing, ending as a runner-up. Spain had beaten France 2-0 in the other semi. Netherlands (Van Dijk, Gakpo, Gravenberch) and Sweden (Isak) fell in the Round of 32 and Germany (Wirtz) went out early.

target.note: Liverpool's priority winger target after Yan Diomande agreed personal terms with PSG (the clubs are yet to agree a deal, so it is not done); Brighton value him around £70–80m and Liverpool could table a concrete offer within a fortnight. Gambia did not qualify for the tournament.

in.meta: LW · from Osasuna · age 22 · hijacked from Newcastle
in.blurb: Spain winger signed from Osasuna on a six-year deal after Liverpool hijacked Newcastle and triggered his €40m release clause (Romano: 'Here We Go', corroborated by Paul Joyce). The summer's first signing.

in.meta: CB · from Rennes · age 20 (agreed Feb, joins now)
in.blurb: Athletic young French centre-back signed from Rennes for ~£55m; a long-term defensive piece.
Teenage defender signed for the future from Austria Wien.
Scotland U16 captain and highly rated centre-back signed from Celtic on a five-year deal (initially scholarship terms; Romano 'Here we go'). One for the academy, joining the U18s for pre-season.

out.blurb: Club legend leaving on a free after a record-breaking spell; 257 goals in 442 games.

out.meta: CB · free → agreed move to Real Madrid
out.blurb: First-choice centre-back leaving on a free, with terms agreed at Real Madrid.
Long-serving left-back departing for Tottenham after nine years and 378 games.
Academy centre-back released after 16 years at the club.

rumIn.meta: RW · Brighton · 21 · priority as Diomande leans to PSG
rumIn.blurb: Brighton's Gambia winger, Liverpool's priority Salah replacement after Yan Diomande agreed personal terms with PSG — though PSG and Leipzig are yet to strike a deal, so that move is not done. Brighton value him at £70–80m and Liverpool are reportedly ready to table a concrete offer within the next fortnight.
rumIn.fitNote: A direct, blistering 1v1 winger who stretches defences and runs in behind — a near-ideal profile for Iraola's vertical, transition-heavy attack, with end product already proven in the Premier League.
rumIn.outlook: The window's marquee winger target with Diomande leaning to PSG; a move may hinge on Man Utd moving first.

wc.note: Gambia did not qualify for the World Cup.

rumIn.meta: RW · Real Oviedo · 24 · bargain clause, Reds 'confident'
rumIn.blurb: Egypt winger who broke out at the World Cup; Liverpool have accelerated their pursuit and are 'confident' of a deal below his €12m (£10.2m) release clause, taking advantage of Oviedo's relegation to the Segunda División. A left-footed right winger and elite dribbler, with Marseille and Celtic also linked.
rumIn.fitNote: A left-footed right winger who cuts inside; ranks in Europe's top 7% for successful dribbles and top 1% for progressive carries — the direct, ball-carrying profile Iraola prizes wide, though the end product (0 goals in LaLiga) and a step up from the second tier are the questions.
rumIn.outlook: A low-cost, high-upside winger option Liverpool are confident of landing; a bargain clause makes it one of the window's most likely deals.

wc.note: Egypt reached the knockouts — Hassan made his World Cup debut off the bench in the R32 win over Australia — before Argentina beat them 3-2 in the Round of 16. Tournament over.

rumIn.blurb: Explosive teenage Köln winger and new Germany international, reported as Liverpool's emerging, cheaper winger alternative; Köln value him around €45–50m.
rumIn.fitNote: A two-footed, pacey winger known for direct dribbling, solo goals and long-range shooting — his aggressive, vertical style suits the system, though a first season in England would be an adaptation.
rumIn.outlook: A high-upside, lower-cost option than the marquee names; genuine competition on either flank if signed.

wc.note: Broke into the senior Germany setup but was not in the World Cup squad.

rumIn.meta: LW · West Ham · 24 · strong WC; Reds join race
rumIn.blurb: West Ham's Dutch winger, impressive at the World Cup (four goal contributions in four games before the Netherlands' Round-of-32 exit to Morocco). Liverpool have joined Chelsea and Man Utd in the race; West Ham value him around £50m, though relegation to the Championship could bring a deal closer to £30–40m.
rumIn.fitNote: A right-footed, direct left winger who cuts inside to shoot and carries in transition — his pace and 1v1 threat fit Iraola's isolate-and-attack wide play, with Premier League end product already proven.
rumIn.outlook: A cheaper, PL-proven winger alternative to Minteh; a relegation-driven discount could make him attractive value.

wc.note: Netherlands OUT in the Round of 32 (lost to Morocco on penalties); Summerville shone with four goal contributions in four games. Tournament over.

rumIn.meta: AM · Almería · 24 · ex-Real Madrid; prolific scorer
rumIn.blurb: Prolific Almería attacking midfielder and former Real Madrid youngster, freshly linked as a cheaper creative option after 25 league goals in Spain's second tier; Almería value him around €25m (£21m), with Benfica and Sporting also keen. Real Madrid hold a 50% sell-on clause from his 2023 sale.
rumIn.fitNote: A left-footed No.10 with a goal-scorer's instinct who thrives between the lines; end product is elite at second-tier level, but the step up in intensity and the pressing demands of Iraola's system are unproven.
rumIn.outlook: A speculative, low-cost creative option; the Madrid sell-on clause complicates the economics.

wc.note: Uncapped at senior level; not in Spain's World Cup squad.

rumIn.blurb: Young Lille midfielder; Liverpool reportedly in contact among other suitors.
rumIn.fitNote: A mobile, ball-winning No.6 who covers huge ground and steps aggressively — ideal to anchor the double pivot behind Iraola's high line, even if attacking output is minimal.
rumIn.outlook: Long-term pivot option; Liverpool among several suitors, deal not advanced.

wc.note: Morocco reached the knockouts (beat the Netherlands on penalties in the R32) but did not make the semi-finals. Tournament over.

rumIn.blurb: Ball-playing Sporting centre-back tipped as the priority Konaté replacement.
rumIn.fitNote: A left-footed, ball-playing CB comfortable defending in space and progressing vertically — suits the high line and quick transitions, with recovery pace key to playing it safely.
rumIn.outlook: Tipped as the Konaté replacement via his release clause; a leading defensive option.

wc.note: Portugal are out — did not reach the semi-finals. Tournament over. Liverpool track him as the priority Konaté replacement.

rumIn.blurb: PSG winger still linked, but PSG's reluctance to sell has cooled the pursuit as Liverpool pivot to Minteh and El Mala.
rumIn.fitNote: A pacey, direct dribbler who thrives running at full-backs in transition — a near-perfect wide weapon for the vertical 4-2-3-1, provided his pressing intensity matches.
rumIn.outlook: Secondary winger target whose pursuit hinges on the Diomande outcome.

wc.note: France reached the semi-finals but lost 0-2 to eventual champions Spain (14 Jul). Tournament over.

rumIn.blurb: Sporting winger floated as a cheaper alternative if Diomande falls through.
rumIn.fitNote: A creative, high-volume chance-creator combining wide directness with clever inside movement — offers the end product and flexibility Iraola prizes wide.
rumIn.outlook: Cheaper fallback if the Diomande deal collapses.

wc.note: Portugal are out — did not reach the semi-finals. Tournament over.

rumIn.blurb: England U21 midfielder who broke out under Iraola at Bournemouth — tenacious, press-resistant and versatile across midfield and the right.
rumIn.fitNote: Already thrived under Iraola at Bournemouth — a high-energy, two-way carrier who covers ground and counterpresses; a plug-and-play fit for the double pivot or right of midfield.
rumIn.outlook: An Iraola favourite and the most system-ready midfield option — a low-risk, known-quantity addition.

wc.note: Uncapped at senior level; not at the World Cup.

rumIn.blurb: Former Liverpool striker now at Al-Hilal, linked with an Anfield return; explosive pace and movement, with finishing consistency the long-standing question.
rumIn.fitNote: Relentless runner who stretches a high line and presses hard — suited to the transition game — but his finishing inconsistency is the question mark for a first-choice No.9.
rumIn.outlook: A speculative reunion; his pace fits the system, though a return would be a gamble on end product.

wc.note: Uruguay are out — did not reach the semi-finals. Tournament over.

rumOut.meta: LW · Tottenham keen; LFC not sanctioning exit
rumOut.blurb: Tottenham remain keen, but Liverpool are not opening the door and are planning for next season with Gakpo in Iraola's squad.

rumOut.meta: CM · Inter Milan opening bid below LFC price
rumOut.blurb: Inter have made an opening proposal that falls short of Liverpool's asking price; interest is concrete but a deal isn't close, with Man Utd also credited with interest.

rumOut.meta: CB · Galatasaray interest; LFC won't sell
rumOut.blurb: Galatasaray interest reported, but Liverpool have no intention of selling their captain.

rumOut.meta: RW · Bayern keen on the teenager
rumOut.blurb: Bayern admire the teenager; Liverpool resisting.
Could move for regular minutes in a World Cup year.

loans.name: Harvey Elliott
loans.notes: Obligation-to-buy reportedly triggered; likely permanent exit
loans.blurb: Creative midfielder on loan at Aston Villa, with an obligation-to-buy reportedly triggered.

loans.name: Kostas Tsimikas
loans.notes: Returning from loan; weighing exit behind Kerkez
loans.blurb: Backup left-back returning from a Roma loan; may move on behind Kerkez.

loans.name: Vítězslav Jaroš
loans.notes: Season loan; LFC contract expires Jun 2026
loans.blurb: Goalkeeper on a season loan at Ajax; contract situation to resolve.

loans.name: Owen Beck
loans.blurb: Young left-back whose Derby loan was reportedly cut short by injury.

loans.name: Luca Stephenson
loans.blurb: Midfielder gaining senior experience on loan in Scotland.

budget.headline: ≈ £150–200m
budget.subtitle: An estimate, not an official figure — funded largely by sales rather than a repeat of last year's ~£450m splurge.

inflows.label: Revenue / PSR headroom
inflows.note: Record £703m 2024/25 revenue (+£89m YoY) and confirmed PSR compliance; Champions League football retained for 2026/27.

inflows.label: Projected player sales
inflows.note: Gakpo (~£75m), Jones (~£35m), Chiesa plus fringe and academy sales — the club is leaning on outgoings to fund spend.

inflows.label: Wage savings
inflows.note: Free departures of Salah, Konaté and Robertson trim the wage bill and create headroom.

committed.label: Muñoz + Jacquet
committed.note: Víctor Muñoz (€40m, Osasuna) and Jérémy Jacquet (£55m, Rennes) are the confirmed arrivals so far.

marquee.label: Yankuba Minteh (target)
marquee.note: Priority winger target after Yan Diomande's ~£86m bid was rejected and the player agreed personal terms with PSG (clubs yet to finalise a deal); Brighton value Minteh at £70–80m, with a concrete Liverpool offer expected within a fortnight. A cheaper alternative in Haissem Hassan (<£10.2m clause) is also advancing. Minteh would dominate the budget if completed.

budget.verdict: Realistic gross outlay of ~£150–200m, but net spend much lower as sale proceeds are reinvested. A marquee winger (Minteh/El Mala, with Diomande leaning to PSG) plus a midfielder and a centre-back is achievable — provided Gakpo and others are sold first.
budget.note: Projection built from public revenue figures, reported bids and likely outgoings. Not an official club budget.

fixtures.opp: Newcastle United (A) — Premier League opener (Iraola's first competitive game)

comparison.subtitle: Both managed in the 2025/26 Premier League — Iraola at Bournemouth, Slot at Liverpool. A statistical preview of the stylistic shift coming to Anfield (FBref / Opta / Understat).
comparison.takeaway: Iraola's Bournemouth pressed far harder (lower PPDA, ~33% more tackles + interceptions) and defended a high line with barely half the ball, while Slot's Liverpool dominated possession and passing but pressed more passively. Both created near-identical xG (~61), but Liverpool defended much better (xGA 47 vs 57). The likely shift: keep Liverpool's control on the ball, but win it back far more aggressively off it.
